entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor L. Moench, the crop studied for its response to drought and fertilization

entities.name: TERMINAL DROUGHT
entities.description: Drought stress occurring during the reproductive stage of the crop

entities.name: NORTH-EASTERN ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The geographic region where the field experiments were conducted

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: A nutrient input (K) used to modulate the crop's response to drought

entities.name: NPSBZN-BLENDED FERTILIZER
entities.description: A blended fertilizer input (BF) containing Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Sulfur, Boron, and Zinc

entities.name: GAS EXCHANGE
entities.description: The process of CO2 and O2 exchange in leaves, used as a physiological indicator

entities.name: DRY MATTER ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The increase in biomass of the plant over time

entities.name: GROWTH PARAMETERS
entities.description: Various measurements used to assess the growth of the sorghum crop

entities.name: NET PHOTOSYNTHETIC RATE
entities.description: The rate of CO2 assimilation (AN) by the plant

entities.name: TOTAL DRY MATTER YIELD
entities.description: The total biomass recorded at physiological maturity

entities.name: LEAF K CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of potassium within the leaves of the plant

entities.name: INTERCELLULAR CO2 CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The concentration of CO2 within the leaf intercellular spaces

entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ratio of biomass produced to the amount of water used (WUE)

entities.name: CROP GROWTH RATE
entities.description: The rate at which the sorghum crop increases in size or mass

entities.name: NET ASSIMILATION RATE
entities.description: The rate of increase in dry weight per unit of leaf area (NAR)

entities.name: NORMAL RAINFALL
entities.description: The control environmental condition without drought stress

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The research method involving controlled trials in the field over two years
